title: Iran's neighbors, under drone attacks, confront their vulnerability
subhead: Lacking experience in countering kamikaze drones, especially the infamous Shahed models, the Gulf countries have shown how unprepared they were for the threat posed by Iran, which is determined to exploit their vulnerability.

title: US says it is nearing 'complete control' of Iran airspace
subhead: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the US and Israel 'will have complete control of Iranian skies, uncontested airspace.' Trump, meanwhile, rated the US performance in the war with Iran as a '15' on 'a scale of 10,' saying the Islamic Republic's leaders were rapidly being killed and that Tehran's arsenal of ballistic missiles was also being 'wiped out.'

title: Macron says he urged Netanyahu to 'refrain from a ground offensive' in Lebanon
subhead: The French president said he also spoke with Lebanon's president and prime minister, stressing the need for the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah to 'immediately cease its attacks against Israel and beyond.'  Meanwhile, the leader of Hezbollah vowed to keep up its fight against Israel, whose forces advanced in Lebanon for a third day.

title: For Lebanon's displaced, a bitter sense of déjà vu
subhead: The government reports that more than 58,000 people have fled their homes since the outbreak of the latest war between Israel and Hezbollah.

title: 'A war justified once again by a lie turns into a race against the clock for Donald Trump'
subhead: The third American war waged in the Middle East by a president of the United States bears little resemblance to the previous ones, observes Le Monde's columnist Gilles Paris.

subhead: After the fall of Bashar al-Assad in Syria and Nicolas Maduro in Venezuela, verbal support is all Vladimir Putin can offer the Iranian government after Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death. However, Moscow could benefit from rising hydrocarbon prices.

title: Israel unleashes unlimited firepower in Iran and Lebanon
subhead: The country launched an operation against Hezbollah in Lebanon on Monday, after the Iranian-allied armed group fired rockets at it. The Israeli military is now seriously engaged in a two-front war.

title: What does international law say about the Israeli-American attack on Iran and Tehran's retaliation?
subhead: Aggression, self-defense, preemptive strikes: The United States, Israel and Iran each justified their military actions, but legal experts question their legality.
